summary:
Claim: The HCO₃⁻/H₂CO₃ buffer system is the best buffer in human physiology due to its pKa proximity to blood pH.  

Evidence: - Its pKa (~6.4) is relatively close to normal blood pH (~7.4), enabling effective buffering near physiological conditions. - The respiratory system dynamically regulates CO₂ levels, adjusting [H₂CO₃] to maintain stable pH through real-time feedback.  

Answer: A
